title: Make parent-skill validator enforce a PER-SKILL routing drift-guard instead of hardcoding the canonical one
rationale: 4a checks only that deep-loop-workflows' own drift-guard exists and 4b skips all non-canonical skills, so a NEW parent skill passes green with inert, unguarded advisorRouting blocks — the comment claims a per-skill guard is authoritative but the code never checks one exists; this is what makes the whole reusable-pattern thesis safe or unsafe

title: Fix stale ai-council @deep-ai-council doc claim and machine-guard the folder!=packetSkillName grandfather via an allowlist
rationale: SKILL.md §7 claims @deep-ai-council is the primary agent identity but the real dispatched agent is @ai-council (registry + all 3 agent files); the grandfather is fine to KEEP (rename is high-regret) but should be corrected in docs and enforced in code so no future skill silently diverges

title: Add a registry discoverability invariant: every mode is lexical/alias-fold OR declares a resolvable command
rationale: 5 of 8 modes are not lexically advisor-scored and 4 of 8 have no advisor map entry; this is mostly correct by design (context stays metadata-routed, improvement lanes are command-gated) but nothing prevents a future mode from being both unscored AND command-less = undiscoverable

title: Keep deep-improvement as one 4-lane packet (do NOT split); optionally sharpen Lane D external-adapter boundary docs
rationale: the four lanes share candidate/dispatcher/scorer seams plus loop-host/reduce-state/promote-candidate with mode-tagged records, so a split would fork shared scripts and raise coupling; Lane D is the only external-adapter outlier but still reuses loop-host dispatch
